description: Four new teacher KPI items extending the existing 11-item teacher_kpi_config in Firestore. Closes the consistency gap where weekly-checklist tasks (lesson plan submission, walkthrough engagement, PLC implementation, bilingual compliance) had no measurable counterpart in the KPI dashboard. Each new item is tagged to Cambridge Teacher Standards 2023, mapped to a competency_framework/teachers id, and linked to the appraisal v2.1 framework item it supports.
rationale: The Eduversal weekly-checklist module (docs/weekly-checklists/) seeds 320 weekly_templates docs with tasks that produce evidence — but four critical evidence categories had no KPI counterpart. Teachers were being asked to submit lesson plans on time, engage with walkthroughs, implement Monday Morning Commitments from PLCs, and meet CLIL bilingual standards — yet none of this was measured at the school KPI level. This extension closes that loop. Cross-references confirmed against the existing modules.
merge_policy: Idempotent. Each new item has a stable id starting with tk_. Re-running the seeder is safe: { merge: true } at the field level preserves any admin edits to the items. Order numbers (12-15) extend the existing order range (1-11) — admin can reorder via the Central Hub /kpi-admin UI.

items.indicator: Lesson plans submitted on time per the Eduversal Lesson Plan Template (5 non-negotiable components)
items.target: ≥95% of weeks with lesson plans submitted by Friday 16:00 deadline using the LPQR-compliant template
items.rationale: Domain 2 of the 6 Domains of Teaching Quality is the precondition for effective Domain 3 delivery. A teacher who plans late or skips the template cannot be supported well. Submission discipline is the most visible Domain 6 indicator and the foundation of every other governance system.
items.first_time_guide: Submit via the school LMS Lesson Plan module by Friday 16:00. Use WALT/WILT framework — WALT describes what students will LEARN, not what they will DO.

items.indicator: Acknowledgement of HoD/Subject Leader walkthrough feedback within 24 hours and visible response in following walkthrough
items.target: ≥90% of walkthrough feedback notes acknowledged within 24h; ≥80% of 'I wonder' reflections visibly addressed in subsequent walkthroughs
items.rationale: Walkthroughs are formative-only; their value depends entirely on the teacher's engagement with the feedback loop. A teacher who never reads or acts on 'I noticed / I wonder' notes has no growth lever. This metric makes the walkthrough rhythm visible as a teacher-side discipline — not just a leader-side activity.
items.first_time_guide: Walkthroughs are NOT appraisal. The 'I wonder' question is a genuine invitation to reflect. Engage with it — the teachers who grow fastest are the ones who treat these as gold.

items.indicator: Specific instructional commitments made at Week-4 Strategy PLC visibly implemented in following week's lessons (verified via walkthrough)
items.target: ≥80% of monthly Monday Morning Commitments observed implemented in next-week walkthrough
items.rationale: PLC engagement without implementation is performance, not learning. The Monday Morning Commitment is the accountability mechanism that converts professional dialogue into instructional change. This metric closes the loop: PLC → commitment → classroom → measured outcome → next PLC.
items.first_time_guide: Your PLC commitment is recorded by your HoD. It must be specific, measurable, observable on Monday. If you forget what it was, ask your HoD or check the PLC minutes.

items.indicator: For English-medium subjects: ≥70% of instructional time delivered in English with appropriate scaffolding for EAL learners
items.target: ≥70% English instruction in designated English-medium lessons (CLIL standard) with bilingual vocabulary support visible
items.rationale: Cambridge Approved Centre status depends on linguistic integrity. A subject designated English-medium that defaults to Indonesian is a compliance breach with student-impact consequences (Cambridge exam preparation gap). CLIL is a pedagogical standard, not just a language preference — content learned through English builds the academic-English skills students need for Cambridge papers.
items.first_time_guide: 70% English means YOUR talk, not student talk. Students may respond in their stronger language while transitioning. Use vocabulary banks, sentence frames, bilingual glossaries — these are scaffolds, not compromises.

weight_audit.comment: Adding 4 items at weight 5 each adds 20 weighted points to the existing 100. Decision required: either rebalance existing weights down OR move to a normalised composite scoring (sum=100 always). Current implementation: extension items added at weight 5 with no rebalancing — admin via /kpi-admin can adjust weights to fit local pilot needs. Cambridge re-approval cycles will eventually require sum=100 normalisation.
weight_audit.recommendation: Phase 1: extension items live at weight 5 each (additive). Phase 2: re-balance to sum=100 by reducing high-weight items (tk_academic_performance 20→15, tk_classroom_engagement 15→12) once dashboards and stakeholders adjust.

provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge Teacher Standards 2023, Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1, Eduversal Teacher Competency Framework, weekly-checklist subject-teacher.json
provenance.operational_pattern_source: Adapted from Semesta School Academic Leadership Governance Handbook 2026-2027 — specifically the gaps the Semesta handbook identified that Eduversal's existing KPI framework did not measure (Section 4 walkthroughs, Section 6 lesson plans, Section 7 PLC commitments, Section 10 CLIL).
provenance.reference_for_audit_purposes: These four items emerged from the cross-module consistency audit run on 2026-05-08 against existing weekly-checklist, appraisal v2.1, and competency framework artefacts. Each item closes a measurement gap that previously existed silently.
